skill.name: nano-banana
skill.description: Generate images with Google Gemini native image models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Gemini 3 Pro Image, Gemini 2.5 Flash Image. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input. Triggers: nano banana, gemini image, gemini 3 pro image, gemini 2.5 flash image, google image generation, native image generation, gemini native image
skill.summary: Generate images using Google Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash models via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: All 40 static findings are false positives. The detected patterns are documentation examples in markdown format, not executable code. Bash commands in code examples demonstrate legitimate CLI usage for the inference.sh tool. URLs are reference documentation links. No prompt injection attempts or security risks detected.

content.user_title: Generate AI Images with Google Gemini Models
content.value_statement: Generate high-quality images using Google Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash models through the inference.sh CLI. Supports text-to-image generation, image editing with multiple inputs, custom aspect ratios, and up to 4K resolution output.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Google Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash models
Edit existing images by providing input image URLs and modification prompts
Generate multiple images in a single request with customizable count
Specify aspect ratios including 1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, and auto
Produce high-resolution output up to 4K with Gemini 3 Pro model
Enable Google Search grounding for real-time information integration
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication before use
External commands are executed through the Bash tool with infsh prefix
Network connectivity is required to access inference.sh API endpoints
Input image editing limited to 14 images per request

use_cases.title: Marketing Visual Creation
use_cases.description: Generate custom visuals for marketing campaigns, social media posts, and promotional materials using detailed text prompts.

use_cases.title: Concept Art and Storyboarding
use_cases.description: Rapidly create concept art, character designs, and storyboard frames for creative projects and pre-visualization.

use_cases.title: Image Modification Workflows
use_cases.description: Edit existing images by adding elements, changing styles, or enhancing content while preserving original composition.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a [subject] in [style] style, with [lighting] lighting
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with simple image generation requests

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create 4 variations of a [subject] with different [artistic style] interpretations
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating multiple options for concept exploration

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image: [image URL]. Change [specific element] to [new element], keep the rest of the image unchanged
prompt_templates.scenario: Modifying existing images while preserving context

prompt_templates.title: High-Resolution Panoramic
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 16:9 aspect ratio image of [scene description] at 4K resolution with photorealistic quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating high-quality panoramic or widescreen images

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a banana floating in space, photorealistic style
output_examples.output: Image generation command executes and returns a URL to the generated image file showing a photorealistic banana floating against a starry space background with proper lighting and shadows.

output_examples.input: Create 4 minimalist logo designs for a coffee shop
output_examples.output: Command generates 4 distinct image URLs, each containing a different minimalist logo interpretation incorporating coffee-themed elements like cups, beans, or steam in clean geometric styles.

best_practices: Be specific and detailed in your prompts to get better results - include style, composition, lighting, and mood descriptors
Use appropriate aspect ratios for your intended use case - 16:9 for landscapes, 1:1 for social media, 9:16 for mobile
Start with Gemini 2.5 Flash for faster iterations, then use Gemini 3 Pro for final high-resolution outputs
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts like 'a nice picture' without specifying style, subject, or composition details
Avoid requesting more images than needed - start with 1-2 images to test prompts before generating larger batches
Do not forget to run infsh login first before attempting any image generation commands

faq.question: What is the difference between Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash?
faq.answer: Gemini 3 Pro produces the best quality images but runs slower. Gemini 2.5 Flash generates excellent quality images quickly. Use Flash for iteration and Pro for final output.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the inference.sh CLI installed. Run npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools to get installation instructions, then run infsh login to authenticate.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, provide image URLs in the images parameter along with a prompt describing the changes. You can include up to 14 input images per request.

faq.question: What image formats and resolutions are supported?
faq.answer: Gemini 3 Pro supports 1K, 2K, and 4K resolutions. Both models support multiple aspect ratios including 1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, and auto.

faq.question: How many images can I generate at once?
faq.answer: Use the num_images parameter to generate multiple images in a single request. This is useful for creating variations of the same prompt.

faq.question: What is Google Search grounding?
faq.answer: Enable Google Search grounding with enable_google_search: true to give the model access to real-time information for more accurate current events or factual visualizations.
